production_note: agentic_K1 dual-LLM explorer: sealed receipt write; packet-only; no bulk writers; no identity stamps
knowledge_boundary: Only landing + prior + active packet content.

exercises.code:
regiones = ["Lima", "Cusco", "Piura"]
for r in regiones:
    print(r)
print(list(range(3)))
exercises.justification_from_packet: for recorre cada elemento; range(3) → [0,1,2] con stop exclusivo (T1-A).
Contador n += 1 para e >= 18; resultado 3 sin comprehension.
Solo ids con monto > 0; 0 y negativos omitidos; lote intacto.
enumerate start=1 para reportes de fila humanos (T1-B).

exercises.code:
nombres = ["Ana", "Luis", "María"]
edades = [30, 25, 40]
for n, e in zip(nombres, edades):
    print(f"{n}={e}")
print("zip corto:", list(zip(nombres, edades[:1])))
exercises.justification_from_packet: zip se detiene en la más corta; el resto se pierde en silencio.
zip_strict lanza ValueError si longitudes difieren — equivalente pedagógico a strict=True.

exercises.code:
lines = ["r1", "r2", "", "r3"]
i = 0
out = []
while i < len(lines):
    line = lines[i]
    i += 1
    if line == "":
        break
    out.append(line)
print(out)
exercises.justification_from_packet: while + centinela vacío: break sin incluir ""; r3 queda fuera del lote.

exercises.code:
intentos = 0
MAX = 3
while intentos < MAX:
    intentos += 1
    print(f"intento {intentos}")
print("done", intentos)
exercises.justification_from_packet: while de reintentos: incrementar intentos evita infinito; imprime done al salir.

exercises.code:
cola = ["job1", "job2", "job3"]
while cola:
    job = cola.pop(0)
    print(job)
    if job == "job2":
        print("PAUSE")
        break
print("restante", cola)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Cola con pop(0); break en job2 deja restante con job3.

exercises.code:
raw_lines = ["  ", "C001|Lima", "SKIP", "C002|Cusco", "END"]
MAX = 100
out = []
n = 0
for line in raw_lines:
    n += 1
    if n > MAX:
        raise RuntimeError("guardrail: demasiadas iteraciones")
    s = line.strip()
    if not s:
        continue
    if s == "END":
        break
    if s == "SKIP":
        continue
    out.append(s)
print(out)
exercises.justification_from_packet: continue vacíos/SKIP, break END, guardrail MAX (demo T2-B).

exercises.code:
i = 0
data = [1, 2, 3]
while i < len(data):
    print(data[i])
    i += 1
print("fin")
exercises.justification_from_packet: Variable de control debe avanzar cada ciclo; sin i+=1 hay loop infinito.
Función que combina continue/break/MAX para intake de líneas.
Contadores por status + primer reject; tasa = n_reject/n_total (T3-A).

exercises.code:
montos = [10, 20, 5, 0]
total = 0
for m in montos:
    total += m
print("total", total, "promedio", total / len(montos))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Acumulador total += m en un for lineal O(n): suma montos del lote sin comprensión, como enseña T3-A.

exercises.code:
ids = ["C001", "C002", "C099", "C003"]
target = "C099"
found = None
for i, rid in enumerate(ids):
    if rid == target:
        found = i
        break
print("found_idx", found)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Búsqueda con break al hallar el id objetivo. Del material activo: Trazar estado es escribir (o imaginar) una tabla: iteración | variables | salida. Es la herramienta #1 para depurar off-by-one y contadores… Por eso en S04-T3-A-E3 se documenta ids = ["C001", "C002", "C099", "C003"] target = "C099"… y la justificación no es genérica.

exercises.code:
results = [
    {"id": "C001", "status": "accept"},
    {"id": "C002", "status": "reject"},
    {"id": "C003", "status": "review"},
    {"id": "C004", "status": "reject"},
]
rejects = [r["id"] for r in results if r["status"] == "reject"]
print("rejects", rejects)
exercises.justification_from_packet: List comprehension para filtrar rejects cuando la transformación es simple.
Set y dict comprehensions del demo: códigos únicos y mapa id→status.
Multi-rama → for explícito, no comprehension densa (T3-B).

exercises.code:
montos = [10, 0, -5, 20]
total = 0
n_pos = 0
print("i | m | total | n_pos")
for i, m in enumerate(montos):
    if m > 0:
        total += m
        n_pos += 1
    print(f"{i} | {m} | {total} | {n_pos}")
print("final total=", total, "n_pos=", n_pos)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Traza de estado iteración|variables para depurar contadores.

exercises.code:
xs = ["a", "b", "c"]
print("len", len(xs), "last_ok_index", len(xs)-1, "last", xs[len(xs)-1])
exercises.justification_from_packet: Último índice válido es len-1; trazar evita off-by-one.

exercises.code:
statuses = ["accept", "reject", "reject"]
n_r = n_t = 0
print("i | st | n_r | n_t")
for i, st in enumerate(statuses):
    n_t += 1
    if st == "reject":
        n_r += 1
    print(f"{i} | {st} | {n_r} | {n_t}")
print("tasa", n_r / n_t)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Traza de tasa reject con denominador n_total de registros procesados.

exercises.code:
xs = ["a", "b", "c"]
linear = sum(1 for _ in xs)
quad = 0
for a in xs:
    for b in xs:
        quad += 1
print("linear", linear, "quadratic", quad)
exercises.justification_from_packet: O(n) vs O(n²): 3 vs 9 pasos con n=3 (T4-B). Según la teoría del paquete (break, continue y prevención de loops infinitos), `break` sale del bucle actual de inmediato. `continue` salta al siguiente ciclo sin ejecutar el resto del cuerpo. En intake: `continue`… El resultado del código lo confirma: xs = ["a", "b", "c"] linear = sum(1 for _ in xs) quad = 0….
xs[len(xs)] lanza IndexError; último ok es len-1.

exercises.code:
ids = [f"C{i:03d}" for i in range(100)]
lookup = set(ids)
print("C050 in set", "C050" in lookup)
print("set membership ~O(1); list ~O(n); evitar doble for innecesario")
exercises.justification_from_packet: Preferir set/dict a doble for para membership en lotes grandes.

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: range(3) produce 0,1,2 — stop exclusivo; list(range(3)) es [0,1,2].
zip sin strict se detiene en la más corta: empareja (1,10) y (2,20); el 3 se pierde en silencio.
La tasa de reject usa denominador n_total de registros procesados/intentados, no solo accepts.
continue salta al siguiente ciclo del bucle (p.ej. filas vacías), sin terminar el programa.
Un doble for anidado sobre n elementos es aproximadamente O(n²).
